title: antennaria parvifolia var. bracteosa
intro: Antennaria parvifolia var. bracteosa, commonly known as dwarf ragwort or Bracted Antennaria, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its low-growing habit and small, white or pinkish flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Antennaria parvifolia var. bracteosa include its potential to support respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
